basics.name: Yujin Bae
basics.label: M.S. Student, KAIST
basics.summary: I am interested in building multimodal large language models that genuinely understand the 3D world. My current work centers on human action understanding, leveraging 3D scene representations to predict and reason about human motion. Building on this, my broader goal is to strengthen robot intelligence with language and multimodality, enabling robots to act reliably and intelligently in the human-centered spaces we live in. My research interests span multi-modal LLMs, egocentric vision, human action anticipation, and human–robot interaction.
